speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Tranio, since for the great desire I had

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: To see fair Padua, nursery of arts,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: I am arrived for fruitful Lombardy,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: The pleasant garden of great Italy,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: And by my father’s love and leave am armed

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: With his goodwill and thy good company.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: My trusty servant well approved in all,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Here let us breathe and haply institute

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: A course of learning and ingenious studies.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Pisa, renownèd for grave citizens,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Gave me my being, and my father first,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: A merchant of great traffic through the world,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Vincentio, come of the Bentivolii.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Vincentio’s son, brought up in Florence,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: It shall become to serve all hopes conceived

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: To deck his fortune with his virtuous deeds.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: And therefore, Tranio, for the time I study

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Virtue, and that part of philosophy

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Will I apply that treats of happiness

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: By virtue specially to be achieved.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Tell me thy mind, for I have Pisa left

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: And am to Padua come, as he that leaves

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: A shallow plash to plunge him in the deep

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: And with satiety seeks to quench his thirst.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Mi perdonato, gentle master mine.

speaker: TRANIO
text: I am in all affected as yourself,

speaker: TRANIO
text: Glad that you thus continue your resolve

speaker: TRANIO
text: To suck the sweets of sweet philosophy.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Only, good master, while we do admire

speaker: TRANIO
text: This virtue and this moral discipline,

speaker: TRANIO
text: Let’s be no stoics nor no stocks, I pray,

speaker: TRANIO
text: Or so devote to Aristotle’s checks

speaker: TRANIO
text: As Ovid be an outcast quite abjured.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Balk logic with acquaintance that you have,

speaker: TRANIO
text: And practice rhetoric in your common talk;

speaker: TRANIO
text: Music and poesy use to quicken you;

speaker: TRANIO
text: The mathematics and the metaphysics—

speaker: TRANIO
text: Fall to them as you find your stomach serves you.

speaker: TRANIO
text: No profit grows where is no pleasure ta’en.

speaker: TRANIO
text: In brief, sir, study what you most affect.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Gramercies, Tranio, well dost thou advise.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: If, Biondello, thou wert come ashore,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: We could at once put us in readiness

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: And take a lodging fit to entertain

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Such friends as time in Padua shall beget.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: But stay awhile! What company is this?

speaker: TRANIO
text: Master, some show to welcome us to town.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Gentlemen, importune me no farther,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: For how I firmly am resolved you know:

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: That is, not to bestow my youngest daughter

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Before I have a husband for the elder.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: If either of you both love Katherine,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Because I know you well and love you well,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Leave shall you have to court her at your pleasure.

speaker: GREMIO
text: To cart her, rather. She’s too rough for me.—

speaker: GREMIO
text: There, there, Hortensio, will you any wife?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I pray you, sir, is it your will

speaker: KATHERINE
text: To make a stale of me amongst these mates?

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: “Mates,” maid? How mean you that? No mates for you,

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Unless you were of gentler, milder mold.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I’ faith, sir, you shall never need to fear.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Iwis it is not halfway to her heart.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: But if it were, doubt not her care should be

speaker: KATHERINE
text: To comb your noddle with a three-legged stool

speaker: KATHERINE
text: And paint your face and use you like a fool.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: From all such devils, good Lord, deliver us!

speaker: GREMIO
text: And me too, good Lord.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Husht, master, here’s some good pastime toward;

speaker: TRANIO
text: That wench is stark mad or wonderful froward.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: But in the other’s silence do I see

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Maid’s mild behavior and sobriety.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Peace, Tranio.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Well said, master. Mum, and gaze your fill.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Gentlemen, that I may soon make good

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: What I have said—Bianca, get you in,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: And let it not displease thee, good Bianca,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: For I will love thee ne’er the less, my girl.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: A pretty peat! It is best

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Put finger in the eye, an she knew why.

speaker: BIANCA
text: Sister, content you in my discontent.—

speaker: BIANCA
text: Sir, to your pleasure humbly I subscribe.

speaker: BIANCA
text: My books and instruments shall be my company,

speaker: BIANCA
text: On them to look and practice by myself.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Hark, Tranio, thou mayst hear Minerva speak!

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Signior Baptista, will you be so strange?

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Sorry am I that our goodwill effects

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Bianca’s grief.

speaker: GREMIO
text: Why will you mew her up,

speaker: GREMIO
text: Signior Baptista, for this fiend of hell,

speaker: GREMIO
text: And make her bear the penance of her tongue?

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Gentlemen, content you. I am resolved.—

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Go in, Bianca.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: And for I know she taketh most delight

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: In music, instruments, and poetry,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Schoolmasters will I keep within my house

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Fit to instruct her youth. If you, Hortensio,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Or, Signior Gremio, you know any such,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: Prefer them hither. For to cunning men

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: I will be very kind, and liberal

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: To mine own children in good bringing up.

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: And so, farewell.—Katherine, you may stay,

speaker: BAPTISTA
text: For I have more to commune with Bianca.

speaker: KATHERINE
text: Why, and I trust I may go too, may I not?

speaker: KATHERINE
text: What, shall I be appointed hours as though, belike,

speaker: KATHERINE
text: I knew not what to take and what to leave? Ha!

speaker: GREMIO
text: You may go to the Devil’s dam! Your gifts are

speaker: GREMIO
text: so good here’s none will hold you.—Their love is

speaker: GREMIO
text: not so great, Hortensio, but we may blow our nails

speaker: GREMIO
text: together and fast it fairly out. Our cake’s dough on

speaker: GREMIO
text: both sides. Farewell. Yet for the love I bear my

speaker: GREMIO
text: sweet Bianca, if I can by any means light on a fit

speaker: GREMIO
text: man to teach her that wherein she delights, I will

speaker: GREMIO
text: wish him to her father.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: So will I, Signior Gremio. But a word, I

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: pray. Though the nature of our quarrel yet never

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: brooked parle, know now upon advice, it toucheth

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: us both (that we may yet again have access to our

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: fair mistress and be happy rivals in Bianca’s love) to

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: labor and effect one thing specially.

speaker: GREMIO
text: What’s that, I pray?

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Marry, sir, to get a husband for her sister.

speaker: GREMIO
text: A husband? A devil!

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: I say “a husband.”

speaker: GREMIO
text: I say “a devil.” Think’st thou, Hortensio,

speaker: GREMIO
text: though her father be very rich, any man is so very a

speaker: GREMIO
text: fool to be married to hell?

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Tush, Gremio. Though it pass your patience

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: and mine to endure her loud alarums, why,

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: man, there be good fellows in the world, an a man

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: could light on them, would take her with all faults,

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: and money enough.

speaker: GREMIO
text: I cannot tell. But I had as lief take her dowry

speaker: GREMIO
text: with this condition: to be whipped at the high cross

speaker: GREMIO
text: every morning.

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Faith, as you say, there’s small choice in

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: rotten apples. But come, since this bar in law

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: makes us friends, it shall be so far forth friendly

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: maintained till by helping Baptista’s eldest daughter

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: to a husband we set his youngest free for a

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: husband, and then have to ’t afresh. Sweet Bianca!

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: Happy man be his dole! He that runs fastest gets the

speaker: HORTENSIO
text: ring. How say you, Signior Gremio?

speaker: GREMIO
text: I am agreed, and would I had given him the

speaker: GREMIO
text: best horse in Padua to begin his wooing that would

speaker: GREMIO
text: thoroughly woo her, wed her, and bed her, and rid

speaker: GREMIO
text: the house of her. Come on.

speaker: TRANIO
text: I pray, sir, tell me, is it possible

speaker: TRANIO
text: That love should of a sudden take such hold?

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: O Tranio, till I found it to be true,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: I never thought it possible or likely.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: But see, while idly I stood looking on,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: I found the effect of love-in-idleness,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: And now in plainness do confess to thee

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: That art to me as secret and as dear

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: As Anna to the Queen of Carthage was:

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Tranio, I burn, I pine! I perish, Tranio,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: If I achieve not this young modest girl.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Counsel me, Tranio, for I know thou canst.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Assist me, Tranio, for I know thou wilt.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Master, it is no time to chide you now.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Affection is not rated from the heart.

speaker: TRANIO
text: If love have touched you, naught remains but so:

speaker: TRANIO
text: Redime te captum quam queas minimo.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Gramercies, lad. Go forward. This contents;

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: The rest will comfort, for thy counsel’s sound.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Master, you looked so longly on the maid,

speaker: TRANIO
text: Perhaps you marked not what’s the pith of all.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: O yes, I saw sweet beauty in her face,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Such as the daughter of Agenor had,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: That made great Jove to humble him to her hand

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: When with his knees he kissed the Cretan strand.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Saw you no more? Marked you not how her sister

speaker: TRANIO
text: Began to scold and raise up such a storm

speaker: TRANIO
text: That mortal ears might hardly endure the din?

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Tranio, I saw her coral lips to move,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: And with her breath she did perfume the air.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Sacred and sweet was all I saw in her.

speaker: TRANIO
text: Nay, then ’tis time to stir him from his trance.—

speaker: TRANIO
text: I pray, awake, sir! If you love the maid,

speaker: TRANIO
text: Bend thoughts and wits to achieve her. Thus it stands:

speaker: TRANIO
text: Her elder sister is so curst and shrewd

speaker: TRANIO
text: That till the father rid his hands of her,

speaker: TRANIO
text: Master, your love must live a maid at home,

speaker: TRANIO
text: And therefore has he closely mewed her up,

speaker: TRANIO
text: Because she will not be annoyed with suitors.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Ah, Tranio, what a cruel father’s he!

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: But art thou not advised he took some care

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: To get her cunning schoolmasters to instruct her?

speaker: TRANIO
text: Ay, marry, am I, sir—and now ’tis plotted!

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: I have it, Tranio!

speaker: TRANIO
text: Master, for my hand,

speaker: TRANIO
text: Both our inventions meet and jump in one.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Tell me thine first.

speaker: TRANIO
text: You will be schoolmaster

speaker: TRANIO
text: And undertake the teaching of the maid:

speaker: TRANIO
text: That’s your device.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: It is. May it be done?

speaker: TRANIO
text: Not possible. For who shall bear your part

speaker: TRANIO
text: And be in Padua here Vincentio’s son,

speaker: TRANIO
text: Keep house, and ply his book, welcome his friends,

speaker: TRANIO
text: Visit his countrymen and banquet them?

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Basta, content thee, for I have it full.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: We have not yet been seen in any house,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Nor can we be distinguished by our faces

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: For man or master. Then it follows thus:

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Thou shalt be master, Tranio, in my stead,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Keep house, and port, and servants, as I should.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: I will some other be, some Florentine,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Some Neapolitan, or meaner man of Pisa.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: ’Tis hatched, and shall be so. Tranio, at once

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Uncase thee. Take my colored hat and cloak.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: When Biondello comes, he waits on thee,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: But I will charm him first to keep his tongue.

speaker: TRANIO
text: So had you need.

speaker: TRANIO
text: In brief, sir, sith it your pleasure is,

speaker: TRANIO
text: And I am tied to be obedient

speaker: TRANIO
text: (For so your father charged me at our parting:

speaker: TRANIO
text: “Be serviceable to my son,” quoth he,

speaker: TRANIO
text: Although I think ’twas in another sense),

speaker: TRANIO
text: I am content to be Lucentio,

speaker: TRANIO
text: Because so well I love Lucentio.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Tranio, be so, because Lucentio loves,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: And let me be a slave, t’ achieve that maid

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Whose sudden sight hath thralled my wounded eye.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Here comes the rogue.—Sirrah, where have you been?

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: Where have I been? Nay, how now, where are you?

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: Master, has my fellow Tranio stolen your clothes?

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: Or you stolen his? Or both? Pray, what’s the news?

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Sirrah, come hither. ’Tis no time to jest,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: And therefore frame your manners to the time.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Your fellow, Tranio here, to save my life,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Puts my apparel and my count’nance on,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: And I for my escape have put on his;

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: For in a quarrel since I came ashore

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: I killed a man and fear I was descried.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Wait you on him, I charge you, as becomes,

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: While I make way from hence to save my life.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: You understand me?

speaker: BIONDELLO
text:
Ay, sir.
                 
                Aside
                .
               Ne’er a whit.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: And not a jot of “Tranio” in your mouth.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Tranio is changed into Lucentio.

speaker: BIONDELLO
text: The better for him. Would I were so too.

speaker: TRANIO
text: So could I, faith, boy, to have the next wish after,

speaker: TRANIO
text: That Lucentio indeed had Baptista’s youngest daughter.

speaker: TRANIO
text: But, sirrah, not for my sake, but your master’s, I advise

speaker: TRANIO
text: You use your manners discreetly in all kind of companies.

speaker: TRANIO
text: When I am alone, why then I am Tranio;

speaker: TRANIO
text: But in all places else, your master Lucentio.

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: Tranio, let’s go. One thing more rests, that

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: thyself execute, to make one among these wooers. If

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: thou ask me why, sufficeth my reasons are both

speaker: LUCENTIO
text: good and weighty.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVINGMAN
text: My lord, you nod. You do not mind the play.

speaker: SLY
text: Yes, by Saint Anne, do I. A good matter, surely.

speaker: SLY
text: Comes there any more of it?

speaker: PAGE
text: My lord, ’tis but begun.

speaker: SLY
text: ’Tis a very excellent piece of work, madam lady.

speaker: SLY
text: Would ’twere done.
